title: Deere: Structural Tailwinds May Lead To Multiple Expansion
text: Deere is trading near ATHs and investors are wondering it is time to lock in some gains. Deere benefits from structural tailwinds. Yet, Deere is still lagging behind peers in terms of recurring revenue. As it builds this new source of revenue, its EPS will become more valuable.

title: Deere Stock Is Too Expensive (Rating Downgrade)
text: Deere & Company has become a very expensive stock, with a market capitalization of almost $130 billion despite a low double-digit P/E. The company is clearly in an up-cycle with its earnings, which has supported its EPS substantially, but there's no guarantee it'll continue. Deere is one of the largest lawn care and heavy equipment manufacturers. We see the company as overvalued versus its long-term earnings risk, making it a poor investment.

title: Deere: Why Buffett Got It Wrong
text: Deere & Company's stock price has more than quadrupled since Warren Buffett sold most of his shares in 2016. The company, which is the largest manufacturer of farm equipment, has benefited from inflation and increased demand for automated solutions in the agricultural industry. Despite risks such as shrinking arable land, Deere & Company is expected to continue growing due to consistent R&D spend and strong demand for its products and technologies.

title: Our Top 10 Picks For Dividend Growth Stocks - July 2023
text: This monthly series of articles focuses on DGI stocks that are likely to provide a high rate of dividend growth rather than a high current yield. We use our proprietary models to rate quantitatively and qualitatively and select the top ten names from an initial list of nearly 400 dividend stocks. A balanced portfolio should have a mix of high-dividend growth stocks and high-yield dividend stocks, depending on the life stage. If you are in the accumulation phase and are several years away from retirement, high-growth dividend stocks would be more appropriate for long-term growth.
